folio.label: 112v
folio.l: Hom to here hous , lustily they ryde
Where as they don , here thynges as he~ list
And whan they saw here tyme , go to rest
¢ Sone after that , this hasty Ianuarie
Wold go to bedde , he wold no lengere tarie
He drynketú , Ypocras , Clarre and vernage
Of spices hote , to encresen his corage
And many a letuarie , had he ful fyne
Swicú as the cursed Monk , Daun Constantyne
Hatú wryten in his bok , de Coitu
To ete hem alle , he was no thyngö eschue
And to hise pryue frendes , thus seide he
For goddis loue , as sone as it may be
Lat voyden al this hous , in curteys wyse
And they han douö , rigút as he wol deuyse
Men drynken , and the trauers drawen a noon
The bryde was brougút , a bedde , as stille as a ston
And whan the bed , was witú the prest I blissed
Out of the chaumbre , hatú euäy wygút hi~ dressed
And Ianuarie , hatú fast in armes take
His fressú May , his paradyse his make
He lulletú hire , he kissetú hire ful ofte
Witú thikke bristelis , of his berd , vn softe
Lyke to the skyn , of houndes fissú , sharp~ as a brere
For he was shaue , al newe , in his manere
He rubbetú hire , a boute hire tendre face
And seide thus , allas , I mot trespace
To ©ow my spouse , and ©ow gretly offende
Er tyme come , that I wol doun descende
But natheles , consideretú this q¶ he
There nys no werkeman , what so euäe he be
That may bothe werke wel , and hastily
This wyl be don at leyser , parfytly
It is no force , how longe that we pleye
In trewe wedlake , coupled be we tweye
And blissed be the ©ok , that we ben Inne
For in actes , we moun do no synne
A man may do no synne , witú his wyf
Ne hurte hiö self , witú his owen knyf
For we han leue , to pleye , as by the lawe
Thus laburetú he , til that the day gan dawe
And tha~ne he taketú a soppe , in fyn Clarre
And vp rigút in his bed , than sittetú he
And after that , he songö ful loude and clere
And kissed his wyf , and mad wantoun chere
He was al coltyssú , ful of ragerye
And ful of Iergouö , as a flekked pye
The slak skyn , a boute his nekke shaketú
